school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: Exactly one of these is true: Carver goes with Period 4; Ellis goes with Library; 79% goes with Period 2.
Between 79% and 85%, one goes with Iris and the other with Liam.
Either Period 4 goes with Cloister or 83% goes with Period 4.
Period 3 goes with at least one of Frost, Greenhouse, or Jonah.
The class in Period 3's grade is exactly 4% more than the class in the Greenhouse's grade.
The class in the Bungalow's period is lower than both the class with Iris' period and the class with 77%'s period.
Bungalow, Piper, and Period 5 belong to different classes.
Ames goes with Iris.
Bungalow goes with neither Jonah nor Liam.
Bungalow goes with either Frost or Piper (but not both).
Dunn's grade is between the class with Piper's grade and the class in Period 1's grade.
Workshop goes with exactly two of Carver, 83%, and Period 3.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: Exactly one of these is true: Boris goes with 2nd; Mikhail goes with London; 1985 goes with 4th.
Between 1985 and 2285, one goes with Fork and the other with Skewer.
Either 2nd goes with French or 2185 goes with 2nd.
3rd goes with at least one of Vera, Italian, or Gambit.
The player who placed 3rd's rating is exactly 200 more than the Italian player's rating.
The Benoni player's placing is lower than both the player who loves the Fork's placing and the 1885-rated player's placing.
Benoni, Zwischenzug, and 1st belong to different players.
